extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won via late-race breakaway bridge at 700m to go, catching solo attacker BETANCOURT after peloton splits in rain
tactical_patterns: Lotto-Soudal forced splits in rain to isolate Viviani, exposing Quick-Step's sprint train weakness in wet technical finales.
Bennett bridged solo at 700m instead of waiting for leadout, revealing sprinters need TT power for wet circuit finishes.
BETANCOURT Carlos attacked solo on circuit lap, nearly held off until final 700m despite no team support.
EF Education First chasing for stage control rather than sprint leadout, showing unconventional aggressive team tactics.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: caught in rear group during rain splits, dislodged from peloton requiring energy-draining chase with depleted team support
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: bridged 700m solo with TT power to catch Betancourt, showing tactical flexibility beyond traditional sprint leadout dependence
rider_signals.BETANCOURT Carlos: positive: solo circuit attack nearly succeeded, showing breakaway riders can exploit wet technical finales against sprinter teams
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: neutral: attempted late attack with Sanchez but overcooked effort before circuit finale
rider_signals.MOHORIC Matej: positive: drove late breakaway selection before Bennett's bridge, demonstrating versatility beyond classics specialist role
